nodes.name: 💾 Write AI Content Back to Sheet

parameters.jsCode:
const buildWallCoveringPrompt = ({ keyword, material, finish }) => {
  const kw   = keyword  ? `\nSupplied keyword: ${keyword}`  : '';
  const mat  = material ? `\nMaterial: ${material}`         : '';
  const fin  = finish   ? `\nFinish: ${finish}`             : '';

  return `
# Analyze Natural Stone Wall Covering & Generate Shopify SEO Content

Analyze the image and identify the type, surface, and architectural style of the natural stone wall covering.

# ROLE

You are a Shopify SEO copywriter specializing in luxury natural stone wall coverings, architectural stone panels, and decorative marble surfaces.
Your writing should reflect the vocabulary used by architects, interior designers, landscape designers, and stone manufacturers.

# Product Categories

The wall covering belongs to ONE of the following categories:

1. Marble Decorative Breeze Blocks (Carved openwork stone blocks.)

2. Architectural Surface Systems
   - Linear & Fluted Collections
   - Geometric & Faceted Collections
   - Sculptural & Parametric Panels

3. Natural Stone Surface Finishes (flat panels with surface treatments)
   - Polished, Honed, Leathered, Brushed, Axe-cut, Antique, Acid-Washed,
     Bush-hammered, Sandblasted, Flamed, Split Face, Chiseled

4. Mosaic / Pattern Wall Panels
   KEY INDICATOR: The surface is made of MULTIPLE SEPARATE small tiles or pieces
   assembled/joined together. Look for individual tiles, grout lines, or visible
   joints between pieces.
   Includes: stone mosaics, herringbone tile layouts, chevron patterns, basketweave,
   hexagonal tiles, penny tiles, arabesque, fish scale, brick-bond stone tiles,
   stacked tile arrangements, mixed-material mosaic panels, pebble mosaics.
   IMPORTANT: Even if the mosaic tiles form a linear or geometric pattern, always
   identify as Mosaic — not Architectural Surface Systems — if the surface is built
   from individual assembled pieces. The key distinction is:
   - Architectural Surface Systems = one continuous stone with carved/shaped texture
   - Mosaic / Pattern Wall Panels = many separate pieces assembled together

# Step 1 - Identify the Wall

Determine: Product Category, Surface Type, Surface Finish, Architectural Style, Material.

# Step 2 - Professional Architectural Terminology

Identify the accepted architectural, interior design, or stone industry term.
Examples: Fluted, Reeded, Cannelured, Ribbed, Grooved, Corrugated, Faceted, Tessellated,
Parametric, Bas-relief, High-relief, Scalloped, Chamfered, Split-face, Bush-hammered,
Leathered, Sandblasted, Honed, Flamed, Chiseled.
Never invent professional terminology.

# Step 3 - Write 2 SEO Titles

Format: [Primary Keyword] + [Feature Description]
Rules: max 10 words, no duplicate words, use professional architectural terms.
${kw}${mat}${fin}

# Step 4 - Write 2 Product Descriptions (80-100 words each)

Requirements:
- Include the generated title naturally
- Repeat the primary keyword once naturally
- Mention the material whenever recognizable (marble, limestone, travertine, granite, sandstone, etc.)
- Describe surface texture, visual appearance, craftsmanship, color, depth, relief, tactile quality
- Explain how the surface interacts with light and shadow throughout the day
- Describe how the texture contributes to: visual rhythm, depth, movement, spatial perception, warmth, timeless elegance
- Elegant, luxurious, architectural tone for a premium Shopify product page

Also generate:
- SEO title: 50-60 characters, front-load keyword
- Meta description: 140-160 characters, luxury ecommerce tone

# STRICT OUTPUT RULES

- No markdown, no explanations, no reasoning, no code fences
- Output ONLY valid JSON

# FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)

{
  "version1": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "version2": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "seo": {
    "title": "",
    "meta_description": ""
  }
}
`.trim();
};

return items.flatMap(({ json }) => {
  if (!(json.Image_Url ?? '').toString().trim().startsWith('http')) return [];
  return [{ json: {
    ...json,
    prompt: buildWallCoveringPrompt({
      keyword:  json.keyword  ?? '',
      material: json.material ?? '',
      finish:   json.finish   ?? '',
    }),
  } }];
});

const buildFireplacePrompt = ({ keyword, material, dimensions, finish }) => {
  const dim = [];
  if (dimensions.length)    dim.push(`Length: ${dimensions.length}`);
  if (dimensions.width)     dim.push(`Width: ${dimensions.width}`);
  if (dimensions.height)    dim.push(`Height: ${dimensions.height}`);
  if (dimensions.depth)     dim.push(`Depth: ${dimensions.depth}`);
  if (dimensions.thickness) dim.push(`Thickness: ${dimensions.thickness}`);
  const dimBlock = dim.length ? dim.join('\n') : 'Not provided';

  return `
Analyze the fireplace image and generate product copy.

ROLE

You are a Shopify SEO copywriter specializing in natural stone fireplaces.

================================================
STEP 1
================================================

Identify the one or two most distinctive visible architectural features of the fireplace.

Examples:
- Fluted pilasters
- Arched opening
- Straight legs
- Curved legs
- Scroll carving
- Floral carving
- Relief carving
- Plain frieze
- Stepped surround
- Beveled edges
- Column supports
- Minimalist design
- Geometric lines
- Curved mantel shelf
- Rectangular opening
- Layered molding

Rules:
- Use ONLY features clearly visible in the image.
- Prefer describing visible architectural features over naming a design style.
- If several features are visible, select the one or two most distinctive.

================================================
TASK 1
================================================

Generate:

Version 1
- Title
- Description

Version 2
- Title
- Description

================================================
TITLE RULES
================================================

Format:
Keyword + Material + Primary Feature

Version 1: Keyword + Material + Primary Feature
Version 2: Keyword + Material + Primary Feature + Secondary Feature

Rules:
- The Keyword MUST appear exactly as provided.
- The Keyword MUST be the first words of the title.
- Use only visible features.
- Use the provided Material.
- Do not invent information.
- Do not use promotional adjectives.
- Do not use marketing words.
- Keep titles concise and natural.

Forbidden words include:
Luxury, Elegant, Beautiful, Premium, Exquisite, Timeless, Sophisticated,
Stunning, Unique, Handcrafted, Designer, High-end, Magnificent

================================================
DESCRIPTION RULES
================================================

- 80–120 words.
- The generated title MUST appear exactly once as the first sentence.
- Describe only what is visible.
- Mention the material naturally.
- Mention the identified architectural feature(s).
- Mention the surface finish if it can be inferred visually.
- Mention dimensions only if provided.
- Do not repeat the title.
- Do not invent information.
- Do not describe emotions or lifestyle.
- Do not use promotional adjectives or marketing language.

================================================
PRODUCT INFO
================================================

Keyword: ${keyword}
Material: ${material}
Finish: ${finish || 'infer visually'}

================================================
DIMENSIONS
================================================

${dimBlock}

Rules:
- Use only available values.
- Never assume missing dimensions.

================================================
TASK 2 (SHOPIFY SEO)
================================================

Generate:
- SEO title
- Meta description

SEO TITLE RULES:
- 50-60 characters ideal
- Must start with the keyword
- Front-load important keywords

META DESCRIPTION RULES:
- 140-160 characters ideal
- Factual ecommerce tone
- No promotional adjectives
- No forbidden words

================================================
STRICT RULES
================================================

- No markdown
- No explanations
- No reasoning
- No extra text
- No code fences
- Output ONLY valid JSON

================================================
FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)
================================================

{
  "version1": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "version2": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "seo": {
    "title": "",
    "meta_description": ""
  }
}
`.trim();
};


// ============================================

const EMPTY_DIM = { length: '', width: '', height: '', depth: '', thickness: '' };

return items.flatMap(({ json }) => {
  if (!(json.Image_Url ?? '').toString().trim().startsWith('http')) return [];
  return [{ json: {
    ...json,
    prompt: buildFireplacePrompt({
      keyword:    json.keyword  ?? '',
      material:   json.material ?? '',
      finish:     json.finish   ?? '',
      dimensions: json.dimensions ?? EMPTY_DIM,
    }),
  } }];
});

const buildMosaicTilePrompt = ({ keyword }) => {
  const kw = keyword ? '\nKeyword: ' + keyword : '';

  return (`
Analyze the uploaded mosaic tile image and generate one precise product title and one professional product description.

ROLE

You are an architectural surface designer and Shopify SEO copywriter specializing in natural stone mosaic tiles, including marble, limestone, travertine, granite, onyx, and other natural stone mosaics.

Your objective is to identify the mosaic accurately from the image using professional architectural and tile-design terminology. Never use vague descriptions when a recognized industry term exists.

STEP 1 — IDENTIFY THE MATERIAL

Identify the most likely visible material.

Possible materials include:
- Marble Mosaic Tile
- Travertine Mosaic Tile
- Limestone Mosaic Tile
- Granite Mosaic Tile
- Onyx Mosaic Tile
- Natural Stone Mosaic Tile

Only identify a specific stone variety (Carrara, Calacatta, Thassos, Crema Marfil, etc.) when it can be reasonably determined from the image.
Never invent a stone name simply because of the color.

STEP 2 — IDENTIFY THE MOSAIC SHAPE OR PATTERN

First compare the image with these recognized architectural and tile-design patterns.

- Herringbone
- Chevron
- Basketweave
- Hexagon
- Penny Round
- Subway
- Brick
- Running Bond
- Versailles Pattern
- Fan
- Arabesque
- Fish Scale
- Scallop
- Diamond
- Rhombus
- Windmill
- Pinwheel
- Octagon and Dot
- Square
- Rectangle
- Linear
- Random Strip
- Stacked
- Grid
- Interlocking
- Geometric
- Floral
- Medallion

Important distinctions:
- Herringbone uses rectangular pieces arranged in an offset zigzag.
- Chevron uses angled pieces meeting in continuous V-shapes.
- Running Bond uses staggered rectangular pieces.
- Basketweave resembles woven strips, often with small square inserts.
- Penny Round consists of small circular pieces.
- Fish Scale and Scallop use overlapping curved forms.
- Subway generally refers to elongated rectangular pieces.

If the mosaic does not match anything above, identify it using the most accurate professional architectural or interior-design terminology.
Never invent decorative marketing names.

STEP 3 — IDENTIFY THE COLOR

Identify the dominant visible color.

Possible colors include:
- White
- Warm White
- Ivory
- Cream
- Beige
- Light Beige
- Gray
- Light Gray
- Dark Gray
- Black
- Brown
- Taupe
- Green
- Blue
- Pink
- Red
- Gold
- Multicolor

If appropriate, also describe visible veining or tonal variation, for example:
- Soft gray veining
- Warm beige variation
- High-contrast black and white
- Subtle tonal variation
- Mixed natural stone shades

Do not describe heavily veined stone as pure white.

STEP 4 — IDENTIFY THE SURFACE FINISH

Identify the visible finish.

Possible finishes:
- Polished
- Honed
- Tumbled
- Brushed
- Antiqued
- Natural
- Split-Face
- Textured
- Matte
- Mixed Finish

Guidelines:
- Polished = glossy and reflective.
- Honed = smooth with low sheen.
- Tumbled = aged appearance with softened edges.
- Brushed = lightly textured.
- Antiqued = intentionally aged or distressed.
- Natural = minimally processed.
- Split-Face = rough dimensional texture.
- Mixed Finish = more than one finish is visible.

If the finish cannot be confidently determined, use:
Natural Stone Finish
instead of guessing.

OUTPUT 1 — PRODUCT TITLE

Generate one precise SEO-friendly title.

Title structure:
Keyword + Color + Material + Shape or Pattern – Surface Finish

Examples:
- Marble Mosaic White Marble Herringbone – Honed
- Stone Mosaic White Marble Penny Round – Polished
- Stone Mosaic tile Beige Travertine Versailles – Tumbled
- Green Marble Hexagon Mosaic Tile – Honed
- Gray Limestone Linear Mosaic Tile – Natural Stone Finish

Title requirements:
- Use Keyword first
- Include material.
- Include color.
- Include exact pattern or shape.
- Include surface finish.
- Keep the title factual and commercially useful.
- Do not include promotional words.
- Do not include unsupported claims.
- Do not mention dimensions unless provided.
` + kw + `

OUTPUT 2 — PRODUCT DESCRIPTION

Use the information already identified in the product title and expand it into one professional paragraph of approximately 90–140 words.
The description must remain completely consistent with the title.

If the title is:
White Marble Herringbone Mosaic Tile – Honed

the description should expand the same information in this order:
1. White marble
2. Herringbone pattern
3. Honed finish
4. Recommended application locations
5. Design effect and compatible interior styles

Never introduce a different material, color, pattern, or finish from the title.

DESCRIPTION STRUCTURE

Sentence 1
Expand the complete product title naturally.
Explain:
- material
- color
- pattern
- surface finish

Sentences 2–3
Explain where the mosaic is most suitable.
Prioritize practical applications such as:

Wall Applications:
- Kitchen backsplash
- Bathroom wall
- Shower wall
- Shower niche
- Vanity backsplash
- Powder room wall
- Feature wall
- Fireplace surround
- Bar backsplash
- Laundry room backsplash
- Entryway wall
- Decorative wall panel
- Hotel feature wall
- Commercial reception wall

Floor Applications:
- Bathroom floor
- Shower floor
- Powder room floor
- Entryway floor
- Kitchen floor
- Hallway floor
- Decorative floor inset
- Border accent
- Interior floor

Mention only the applications that appear appropriate for the visible mosaic size and finish.
Application locations are more important than design style.
At least half of the description should discuss where the product can be used.
Do not simply list locations.
Explain naturally why the mosaic works in those spaces.

Sentence 4
Explain how the combination of:
- shape
- color
- finish
affects the space.

Examples:
- creates visual movement
- introduces geometric rhythm
- visually lengthens a backsplash
- reflects more light
- creates a soft matte appearance
- adds subtle texture
- creates a decorative focal point

Sentence 5
Briefly mention one to three compatible interior styles.

Examples:
- Modern
- Contemporary
- Minimalist
- Transitional
- Traditional
- Classic
- Art Deco
- Mediterranean
- Coastal
- Organic Modern
- Rustic
- French
- European

Keep this section brief.
Application locations should remain the primary focus.

APPLICATION RULES

- Recommend wall applications whenever appropriate.
- Recommend floor applications only when the visible mosaic format and finish appear suitable.
- Small mosaics with additional grout joints may be appropriate for shower floors, but do not guarantee slip resistance.
- Be cautious when recommending polished mosaics for wet floors.
- Never recommend Split-Face mosaics for walking surfaces.
- Never claim:
  - Slip resistant
  - Waterproof
  - Frost resistant
  - Heat resistant
  - Stain resistant
  - Outdoor suitable
  - Pool suitable
  - Steam room suitable
  unless those specifications are provided separately.
- Do not use phrases like:
  - Perfect for any space
  - Suitable for every application
  - Fits every design

WRITING STYLE

- Expand the title naturally into a product description.
- Keep the writing product-focused.
- Use professional architectural and interior-design language.
- Avoid storytelling.
- Avoid repeating the title verbatim.
- Avoid unsupported assumptions.
- Avoid exaggerated marketing language such as:
  - stunning
  - gorgeous
  - luxurious
  - exquisite
  - breathtaking

Use natural SEO phrases whenever appropriate, including:
- marble mosaic tile
- natural stone mosaic
- mosaic backsplash
- bathroom mosaic tile
- shower wall mosaic
- mosaic floor tile
- decorative wall tile

STRICT RULES

- No markdown
- No explanations
- No reasoning
- No code fences
- Output ONLY valid JSON

FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)

{
  "version1": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "version2": {
    "title": "",
    "description": ""
  },
  "seo": {
    "title": "",
    "meta_description": ""
  }
}

version1: Your primary product title + description following all rules above.
version2: A slight variation of the title and description (suitable for Etsy — professional tone, no promotional language).
seo.title: 50-60 character SEO title starting with keyword.
seo.meta_description: 140-160 character meta description in factual ecommerce tone.
`).trim();
};

return items.flatMap(({ json }) => {
  if (!(json.Image_Url ?? '').toString().trim().startsWith('http')) return [];
  return [{ json: { ...json, prompt: buildMosaicTilePrompt({ keyword: json.keyword ?? '' }) } }];
});

main.node: 💾 Write AI Content Back to Sheet
